data.epi_content:
I used to be so afraid, but now I realize that simply crossing a line can resolve things so easily.

I didn't even need to kill anyone.

I've realized that for someone who thinks they're smart but freezes when their life is threatened, just a little intimidation is enough.

When I pressed the still-warm gun barrel against Lydia's cheek, she began to wet herself, just like I had screamed in fear when locked in that dark locker.

Finding it somewhat—no, extremely—disgusting, I stepped away from the crumpled Lydia and kicked her in the solar plexus, as if kicking away a garbage bag.

Though she's coughing and suffering, she's still breathing fine, which only confirms how fragile this body truly is.

"Sigh, the tobacco tasted so good until just now.

I had to throw it all away because of you. What should I do about that?"

"P-please spare me...!"

"Oh, I won't kill you. But you're not in any position to ask me for anything.

You're the supposedly high-and-mighty daughter of the Mecklenburg family, some countess or whatever, just a worthless byproduct of pathetic trash.

Who knows, maybe your seed didn't even come from the count but from some handsome male prostitute. You don't look like your father at all."

When I insulted her, Lydia momentarily tried to stand up in anger, but then looked at the modern weapon in my hand and lowered her eyes again.

Violence truly is the solution to everything, isn't it?

I had been sitting quietly, hoping my situation would resolve itself through words and appropriate actions.

But no one listened to me, everyone was busy ignoring me, and this worthless friend in front of me was busy tormenting me.

Feeling irritated, I kicked Lydia's face as she sat there.

Her head hit the wall with a slight crushing sound, and Lydia began bleeding from her nose.

She grabbed her nose and started sobbing, her shoulders trembling.

"The sound of your sniffling is giving me a headache.

Lydia, what if I accidentally pull the trigger?"

At that, Lydia stopped sniffling and closed her mouth, looking at me with frightened eyes, trembling violently.

"Don't tremble, don't tremble. I'm not scary.

This friend here is the scary one."

Each time the gun barrel grazed her face, Lydia trembled severely.

I decided to tell her an interesting story to help her relax.

After all, even teachers sometimes tell interesting stories to lighten the mood during class.

As someone teaching Lydia manners and etiquette, I should help her relax too.

"You know, I was cooped up in my room all day thinking about various things.

And I had a revelation! Ah, if complex conversations don't work, I should use the most primitive means of communication."

Saying that, I lowered the hammer.

The sound was so much like a bullet about to fire that Lydia couldn't stop trembling.

She wasn't responding, it wasn't fun, and she wasn't even listening to me properly.

"Don't make a sound."

I took the still-burning tobacco in my left hand and rubbed it against the spot where Lydia had been shot.

The unique smell of cooking flesh and blood rose up.

It's quite impressive how the girl in front of me is trying to hold back her tears.

If life is so precious to her, I wish she had realized that other people's lives are precious too.

"Lydia. Don't come looking for me anymore.

It's not a bad idea to try to bring people to harm me, but I'll at least blow your head off before I go."

"I... understand..."

What do you understand?
data.epi_content2:
Thwack.

The handle met the delicate girl's philtrum once more.

"Don't answer. I don't particularly want to hear your voice."

She's not answering.

How insolent.

It's common sense that when someone superior speaks, you should respond politely.

People who don't observe manners, etiquette, common sense, and norms are no better than beasts.

And beasts only listen when you beat them.

So I diligently educated and disciplined Lydia with my already weak body.

With just a slight, minimal punishment.

Like civilizing those who have human form but lack proper beliefs, don't use human language, and have no common sense or concept!

"It's tiring. You're always standing in my way and interfering with me."

After kicking her for quite some time, Lydia grabbed my leg with trembling hands as if begging me to stop, so I struck her face again with the handle.

Blood splattered around, making my clothes a bit dirty, but I'll consider this a sacrifice for Lydia.

Considering the valuable lesson and the etiquette that will become ingrained in her, this is a small price to pay.

I truly am a genuinely merciful person.

She's learned one thing: no matter how much you beg someone to stop bullying you, they won't stop.

It took me dying twice to realize that fact.

I'm literally sharing wisdom gained at the cost of my life, so I'm truly a great teacher.

"Pl...please...!"

Lydia, whose face is now so reshaped that it's unrecognizable, shows off her newly beautified red face while grabbing my leg.

But I didn't give permission for her to touch my body.

"Lydia, I'm quite busy and have very little time to spare for you.

Did you think I wouldn't know why you came looking for me? Did you think I wouldn't know why you brought someone with you?"

Yes, I'm very busy.

I have to sit and space out, lean against the balcony railing to watch people passing by, smoke lots of cigars, and occasionally play the game of making holes in various parts of my body like the one on my instep...

I have a lot to do.

"I'm...I'm sorry!"

As Lydia spoke urgently, the blood pooled in her mouth splattered onto my face.

How unpleasant.

As I was about to step on the fallen Lydia, I suddenly felt people gathering around.

Looking around, I saw several children watching me and whispering.

While I was grinding Lydia's head under my foot, someone with a strong sense of justice addressed me.

"I don't know exactly what's happening, but wouldn't it be better for everyone if you stopped there?"

I didn't bother turning my head toward the voice.

It wasn't worth facing someone for this conversation.

"I don't know about that."

"Then I'll have no choice but to stop you by force."

The person behind me approached with the sound of drawing a sword from their waist.

In the end, it's all about power.

With enough force, anything is permitted.

Dealing with the aftermath is another matter.
data.epi_content3:
It's not like I killed Lydia; I just hit her a little during an argument. Who would say anything about that?

Children living in the Emperor's enclosure are full of vigor and often fight.

As a result, punishments are limited to cleaning somewhere or being confined to a room for a few days.

It's a world quite lenient toward violence.

Well, in a world where limbs can be reattached, disinfected, and healed with a spell, there's no need to be strict about violence.

Anyway, after such idle thoughts, I turned around fearing I might get stabbed in the back, but it wasn't a person.

"Wow, these days even livestock wears clothes and pretends to be knights."

At those words, the human who had drawn the sword frowned.

The words left my mouth before I knew it.

Me, no, not me. Erica. No, it is me after all.

Yes, I just blurted out what I thought upon seeing something in front of me.

Judging by his handsome face despite his skin color, could it be that friend?

The mixed-race knight who appeared to be on friendly terms with Vivian.

"It's just two silly girls fighting and squabbling. Do you need to intervene so violently with a sword?"

"But this isn't a fight; it's just one-sided violence, so I must intervene.

Unnecessarily tormenting someone who isn't resisting is a cowardly act."

The man answers with a sharp voice.

As if he finds it absurd.

But why is he pretending to be human while showing emotions?

It's baffling.

The human standing before me is that kind of being.

Like a kind of Minotaur...

Either some crazy man thought of mounting a cow and acted on it, or some crazy cow attacked a woman, resulting in such a being.

With each step this alien something takes toward me, confusion fills my mind.

But I didn't show it.

"So, where's your owner?"

"What owner are you suddenly talking about?"

"The person who ordered you to do this."

At those words, the man's eyes widened slightly as if hurt, and he raised his sword.

"Remove your foot now."

"So, instead of relaying such messages, tell your owner to come directly... ugh."

Without even listening to the end of my sentence, he approached me, pushed me lightly with his hand, and began supporting Lydia to take her away.

He seemed slightly surprised, as if he hadn't expected me to fall on my backside.

He hesitated briefly, then tried to help me up with an unnecessarily dignified posture.

Disliking his touch, I pushed myself up from the corridor floor covered in Lydia's blood.

"Keeping a Black in a building with a sword instead of in a cotton field.

Your owner must be out of their mind. If you ever want to return to your proper place, just say so.

I'll send you back to the plantation immediately.

How could they put such a filthy thing as the male protagonist?

The author must be out of their..."

I fled into my room.

My thoughts, which had been spinning like a lady's, return to normal.
